title: Topical Anaesthetic Cream (LMX4)
content_markdown:
##### Type

Topical anaesthetic

##### Presentations

5g tube of cream containing lignocaine 40mg/g

##### Actions

Local topical anaesthesia 
 Onset: 30 minutes 

#### Uses

Topical anaesthesia prior to anticipated IV cannulation in paediatric patients.

#### Adverse Effects

- Irritation

- Redness

- Itching

- Rash

- Anaphylaxis (rare)

- Corneal irritation (accidental eye exposure)

#### Contraindications

- Amide local anaesthetic hypersensitivity

- Broken skin (atopic dermatitis, wounds, mucous membranes)

- Infants < 3 months old

#### Precautions

- Eye contact

- Excessive dosing (large area and prolonged application time)

- Severe hepatic, renal or cardiac impairment

- Anticipated need for IV access within 30 minutes

#### Pregnancy and Breastfeeding

##### Pregnancy

- Safe to use

##### Breastfeeding

- Safe to use

#### Doses

##### Paediatric only 

- 1 g cream = 5cm cream 
- 3-12 months: ≤ 1g per site – 3 sites only (2 x ACF, 1 dorsum of hand)
- ≥ 12 months: 1g – 2.5g per site
- Do not exceed 10g total application

#### Special Notes

- Pain, anxiety, distress and subsequent needle phobia is associated with IV access attempts. Preparation of potential IV cannulation sites with topical anaesthetic cream can reduce pain and minimise distress to patients.
- If skin irritation occurs, remove the cream immediately.
